# Democraft

Democraft turns real browser workflows into deterministic Remotion videos. A
human developer and an LLM author the same TypeScript API; there is no separate
agent format.

## Public packages

- `@democraft/core`: authoring API, targets, locators, and custom visuals.
- `@democraft/compiler`: compilation, validation, and readable inspection.
- `@democraft/playwright`: browser capture and recorded manifests.
- `@democraft/timeline`: frame-accurate timeline resolution.
- `@democraft/remotion`: React composition and MP4 rendering.
- `@democraft/schema`: portable artifact types and `DCxxxx` diagnostics.
- `@democraft/cli`: the `democraft` command.

## Minimal authoring pattern

```ts
import { byTestId, defineDemo, defineTargets } from "@democraft/core";

const targets = defineTargets({
  dashboard: byTestId("dashboard"),
});

export default defineDemo({
  id: "dashboard-tour",
  title: "Dashboard tour",
  source: { baseUrl: "http://localhost:3000" },
  targets,
  async run({ demo }) {
    await demo.scene("intro", async (scene) => {
      await scene.goto("/dashboard");
      await scene.expectVisible("dashboard");
      await scene.establish("dashboard");
      await scene.caption("Everything you need, at a glance.");
    });
  },
});
```

Keep a demo self-contained unless shared targets or visual components genuinely
benefit from another file.

## Preferred CLI workflow

The demo path is optional when `demo.ts`, `demo.tsx`, `src/demo.ts`, or
`src/demo.tsx` is unambiguous.

```bash
pnpm exec democraft validate demo.ts
pnpm exec democraft inspect demo.ts --json
pnpm exec democraft targets demo.ts --json
pnpm exec democraft render demo.ts -o demo.mp4
pnpm exec democraft studio demo.ts
```

Use individual artifact stages only for CI, debugging, or deliberate reuse:

```bash
pnpm exec democraft capture demo.ts --output-dir .democraft/runs/dashboard-tour
pnpm exec democraft timeline demo.ts \
  --manifest .democraft/runs/dashboard-tour/manifest.json \
  -o .democraft/timelines/dashboard-tour.json
pnpm exec democraft render demo.ts \
  --manifest .democraft/runs/dashboard-tour/manifest.json \
  --timeline .democraft/timelines/dashboard-tour.json \
  -o demo.mp4
```

## Custom visual components

Declare React/Remotion-compatible components in the same demo module. Props and
visual IDs are inferred by TypeScript.

```ts
import { defineDemo, defineVisual } from "@democraft/core";
import { LaunchTitle } from "./launch-title";

export default defineDemo({
  id: "launch",
  title: "Launch",
  source: { baseUrl: "http://localhost:3000" },
  visuals: {
    "local.launch-title": defineVisual(LaunchTitle),
  },
  async run({ demo }) {
    await demo.scene("intro", async (scene) => {
      await scene.visual("local.launch-title", { title: "New analytics" });
    });
  },
});
```

Components may be local or copied from a shadcn-style registry such as remocn.
The user owns the copied source. A custom Remotion entry is only for replacing
the whole composition or other advanced renderer behavior.

## Agent rules

1. Prefer semantic target IDs and resilient locators: role, label, test ID, text.
2. Run `validate` before capture or render and repair every error diagnostic.
3. Prefer the high-level `render` and `studio` commands over manual pipelines.
4. Use `--json` for machine-readable inspection, target lists, and diagnostics.
5. Treat `.democraft/` as generated artifacts, never as authoring source.
6. Presentation-only changes can reuse capture; changed browser actions or
   locators require a new capture.
7. Do not hand-author Demo IR. Author `demo.ts`; the compiler owns the IR.

## More context

- `README.md`: product overview and source quickstart.
- `apps/docs/content/sdk/overview.mdx`: authoring API.
- `apps/docs/content/cli/overview.mdx`: CLI reference.
- `apps/docs/content/concepts/components.mdx`: user-owned visual components.
- `docs/architecture/pipeline.md`: artifact pipeline internals.
